evidence_stage.final_test_basis: Final MECH-057 adjudication requires integrated behavioral evidence with responsibility-bearing intervention traces, not toy proxy runs alone.
evidence_stage.proxy_interpretation: Current MECH-057 probes are proxy mechanistic pressure tests and should not be read as final ethical-agency verdicts.

items.description: Extract evidence from six key papers to assess whether basal ganglia implement three distinct gating loops (sensorium/theta-thought/action-commitment) or a single action gate with three criteria. Papers: O'Reilly & Frank 2006 (PBWM), Hazy/Frank/O'Reilly 2007, Aron et al. 2007 (STN hyperdirect), Brittain & Brown 2014 (beta oscillations), Buckner et al. 2008 (DMN), Crick 1984 / Zikopoulos & Barbas (reticular nucleus thalamus).
papers_to_extract: O'Reilly & Frank (2006) Making working memory work — PBWM model, BG gates PFC WM via Go/NoGo
Hazy, Frank, O'Reilly (2007) Towards an executive without a homunculus — three-loop BG hierarchy
Aron et al. (2007) Triangulating a cognitive control network — STN hyperdirect, beta suppression
Brittain & Brown (2014) Oscillating to a halt — beta as hold signal, STN beta and status quo
Buckner et al. (2008) The brain's default network — mPFC-hippocampal axis, DMN suppression by task
Crick (1984) / Zikopoulos & Barbas — reticular nucleus as inter-loop thalamic gating switch
items.title: Three-Gate BG Architecture: Literature Extraction (Q-019)
items.user_notes: Pinned: survives backlog regeneration. Do not auto-delete.

manually_appended.next_action: Run targeted lit-pull: source monitoring (Johnson-Raye tradition), frontoparietal reality monitoring, internal vs external source attribution in memory encoding, corollary discharge and efference copy in self/other distinction. Target: Mitchell 2009, Simons et al. 2008, Frith 1992/2014 on corollary discharge.
manually_appended.added_note: Registered following Papez circuit lit-pull discussion: Schnider 2003 maps to temporal context filtering not simulation/real distinction; source monitoring is the more direct MECH-094 implementation candidate.

manually_appended.next_action: Run targeted lit-pull: ACh/NE neuromodulation of hippocampal encoding mode (Hasselmo 1999/2006), state-dependent LTP/LTD, encoding vs retrieval mode switching, waking/REM/SWS neuromodulatory profiles and their effects on hippocampal write probability.
manually_appended.added_note: Registered following identification of action-mode write-profile gap: neuromodulatory mode-setting is the primary biological mechanism for mode-specific hippocampal encoding, not a categorical tag.

manually_appended.next_action: Run targeted lit-pull: habitual vs deliberate action hippocampal write profiles -- hippocampus-bypass in habit (striatal dominance), encoding fidelity during goal-directed vs automatic action, transition between model-based and model-free learning and its hippocampal correlates.
manually_appended.added_note: Papez lit-pull established circuit anatomy but did not address habitual vs deliberate vs simulated mode write-profile differences. This is the most significant remaining gap in the Papez/ARC-007 evidence.

manually_appended.next_action: Run targeted lit-pull: signal-degradation / pareidolia models of psychosis. Targets: sleep deprivation and perceptual distortion, stimulant-induced psychosis phenomenology (preserved insight, signal-dependent), early psychosis as-if quality and threshold alterations, apophenia models (Conrad 1958 tradition). Distinguish from precision-failure psychosis (MECH-244) by preserved insight and signal-dependency.
manually_appended.added_note: Registered from clinical discussion: pareidolia / signal-degradation pathway to psychotic symptoms identified as distinct from Adams/Friston precision-failure model.

manually_appended.next_action: Run targeted lit-pull: trauma-shaped priors and psychosis. Targets: trauma-organized psychotic ideation, paranoid ideation in complex PTSD, pseudo-hallucinations in PTSD, Bayesian accounts of threat hypervigilance, prior probability distortion from traumatic experience. Distinguish from MECH-244 (neurochemical precision failure) by content-specificity and trauma-organization of symptoms.
manually_appended.added_note: Registered from clinical discussion: trauma-shaped hypervigilant priors identified as a distinct pathway to psychotic symptoms with different therapeutic implications from MECH-244.
